These are basic cotton eczema gloves but they pass the only test I care about: I can slather on a really thick hand cream at night and still get the gloves on without fighting them, and they actually stay on while I sleep. The fingers are long enough for me (average woman hands) so I’m not yanking at the tips all night, and the wrist is snug enough to not slide off but not so tight it leaves marks.

Material wise they’re soft and not scratchy, and they don’t feel like they’re trapping heat in a weird way. After a few washes they’ve held up fine (no seams splitting and they didn’t turn into a stretched-out mess), and the lotion comes out in the laundry without leaving them feeling coated. For what they are, they’re easy to grab, throw on, and keep in rotation, and having 6 pairs is nice so you aren’t stuck doing laundry constantly just to use them again.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Diet Drops for Weight Loss for Women & Men (2 Fl Oz) Made in USA- Liquid Weight Loss Drops with L Carnitine, L Arginine, L-Glutamine Supplement, African Mango Extract Drops
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
I am guessing 1 serving is the "10 drops" recommended, and then you need to take this 3 times a day. The bottle lists 120 servings so that gives you, ruffly, a 40 day supply. I have no way to explain the flavor beyond gross and a lingering metallic/chemical taste that permeates everything you put in your mouth for hours.

So if making everything you put in your mouth taste horrid then yes 100% this will totally suppress your desire to eat. Otherwise save your money and just don't ingest this.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
The deciding factor for me with these was taste, and it’s a hard no. It has that strong herbal/supplement flavor and the aftertaste hangs around way too long. I tried it straight, chased it with water, and even mixed it into a drink and I could still taste it. If you’re someone who can tolerate “medicinal” drops daily then you might be fine, but if you’re even a little picky this is going to be a compliance problem.

Because I wasn’t willing to dread it every day, I can’t honestly speak to any real “weight loss” results. I’m not going to pretend I saw a big change when I wasn’t consistent with it.

For the money, it just isn’t worth it for me. It’s a small 2 oz bottle and depending on how many drops you’re taking per day you can burn through it pretty fast, and if you don’t like the taste you’ll end up with an expensive bottle sitting in the cabinet. I won’t be re-buying, but if you’re fine with strong tasting liquid supplements then it may work better for you than it did for me.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Gel Pain Relief Insoles Silicone Anti Slip Pads Durable & Comfortable Cushion Inserts, 1 Pairs Transparent
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
These are ultra tiny shoe inserts. I wear a size 9 (to 9 & 1/2) and these Stop before reaching the base of my big toe. They are not wide enough for my shoes. These are probably designed for, at most, a US women's size 6 shoe.

The gel itself is nice and comfortable but sadly not usable by anyone in my family unless cutting down to fit into children's shoes.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are basic clear gel/silicone pads and they really only do 2 things for me: add a little cushion and stop that annoying “foot sliding forward” in flats/loafers. The catch is they ONLY work if they actually fit the area you’re trying to cover.

I tried them in two pairs of shoes. In my roomier flats they were perfect and stayed put, and I could immediately tell the difference because my toes weren’t getting jammed up at the front. In a narrower toe box they were basically a no-go because they bunched up and felt weird under my foot, so the “anti slip” benefit didn’t matter since the fit was wrong.

Comfort-wise they’re soft and not irritating (no sharp edges), and they’re clear so they don’t look odd in the shoe. For the price, if you have shoes that you know you slide around in and the pad fits your shoe shape, they’re worth it. If your shoes are tight up front, I’d skip because the sizing/shape is the whole gate with these.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: BAKIPACK 30 Gift Boxes with Lids
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are thin gift boxes.  No to say they are cheap just that they are very light weight.  They fold easily and have a nice shimmery rose gold color to them.  Takes no time to assemble and they are really cute.  If you put something heavy in them, you will need to support the bottom.  Light weight items, like jewelry won't be an issue.  A cupcake would probably be the weight limit without keeping my hand under the bottom.  Overall, perfect simple gift or display box.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are cute little gift boxes and I was honestly worried a 30 pack would show up smashed, but mine arrived in good shape (no crushed corners). They fold up pretty quick and once you get the first one done the rest go fast. Lids fit right and don’t pop off when you pick the box up.

Just be realistic about what they’re made for. The cardboard is on the thinner/lighter side, so these are best for small/light gifts (jewelry, candy, small skincare items, gift cards, etc). I did a quick “weight check” with a small paperback and it held up fine sitting still, but I wouldn’t use these for anything heavy where you’re expecting the box to carry the load without flexing.

Overall they’re easy to assemble, look nice once put together, and for light gifts they do exactly what I needed.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Stainless Steel Scouring Pad Set, 4 Stainless Steel Scourers with 4 Cleaning Sponges, for Dish Washing, Kitchen Cleaning, Cooking Utensil, Cookers, Tableware, Kitchenware, Household Ware (8 Packs)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
These are decent sponges & scouring pads. The stainless steel is pretty much normal standard quality you will find available at any grocery or discount "dollar" store. They hold up rather well and do a pretty great job on your heavy duty pots.

The sponges are another issue though. They are ultra light and not very durable. The yellow portion feels similar to a car sponge while the green is just okay. They aren't the worst sponges I have ever used. They do rinse out and dry easily without molding super fast. But they just don't last very long with multiple daily use.

For the price these aren't the best.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These do the job, but for the price I don’t think they’re the best set unless you just need something basic to burn through.

My quick test was a stainless pot with dried on rice/cheese and a greasy cookie sheet. The steel scrubbers cut through the stuck-on bits fast (a short soak still helps for the really baked on stuff) and they didn’t instantly unravel or shed a bunch of sharp wires all over the sink, which is the main thing I worry about with cheap steel pads.

The weak link for me is the sponges. The green scrub side is fine at first, but with multiple daily dishes they start looking rough pretty quickly compared to the grocery store ones I normally buy. So even though you’re getting 8 pieces total, the “value” isn’t amazing because I’ll end up replacing the sponges sooner than I want.

Overall: steel pads = solid and effective, sponges = just ok. I’ll use them up, but I probably wouldn’t repurchase this exact set at full price.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: DoPake King Size 110 mm 50 Pack Pre-Rolled Cones Rolling Paper with Tips & Packing Sticks Included (110 mm/ 4.3inch) (Yellow)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are the cone shaped with a folded 'filter' like tip.  It isn't a true filer but filter like.  The paper is unbleached, it has that light brown or old paper look to it but will burn cleanly and not have a ton of black smoke.  Since these are pre-rolled if you like chunky or very loose tobacco / herb then this is a super easy fill for you otherwise you will need to ultra grind your flavor and then carefully funnel it in then pack it as you go along.  It is time consuming but it you basically have a log of a roll when done.  These don't come in a tin but overall are super high quality and easy to use.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These cones are pretty much what I wanted for convenience, BUT they’re not totally “zero effort” like some people assume. They are 110mm king size, come pre-rolled with the tips already in, and the packing sticks are actually the right size so you’re not hunting around for a pen/clip to tamp it down. If you’re buying cones because you don’t want to roll, this part is legit.

Couple real-world notes after filling a bunch of them: you still need to take your time packing or you’ll get runs/canoeing. If you pack too tight they’re hard to draw, too loose and they burn uneven. I ended up doing a little fill/tamp in layers and that fixed most of it, but it’s not a “dump it in once and go” situation unless your grind is perfect.

Paper feels like normal thin rolling paper and they didn’t fall apart on me while packing, which is my main veto point with cheap cones. Burn was clean enough in my use (no weird dark/sooty smoke), and I didn’t notice any nasty chemical taste.

One small complaint: a few cones in my pack were slightly misshapen at the tip so they didn’t stand up well while filling, so I had to babysit those more. Overall though, if you just want a consistent, low-hassle cone that comes with the sticks, these do the job.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Natural Sensitive Toothpaste (3PK) - Fluoride Free - Toothpaste for Sensitive Teeth and Gums - Vegan, SLS Free, No Artificial Flavor, Sweetener, Color - Healthy Tooth & Gum Oral Care
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
Most toothpaste, to me, has a sweet / sugary kind of flavor mixed in. This toothpaste doesn't really have that. It isn't bitter or chemical just has a somewhat minty non overly sweet flavor. Not a ton of foam, which I don't think is an issue, and does leave my mouth/teeth feeling clean. Overall, this doesn't taste bad at all but it is different. If you have ever had "natural" or "chemical free" (I didn't label it that) toothpaste then you will get the idea. Kids may not be to excited to use this.

Price pint, per tube, is a bit higher then standard commercial brands but they are pretty normal / comparative to other natural toothpastes I have used.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I grabbed the 3 pack because we go through toothpaste fast in this house and I specifically wanted fluoride free without a bunch of extra junk (SLS, artificial sweeteners/colors, etc).  First thing I check with “natural” pastes is if I can actually tolerate the taste twice a day, because if it has that weird lingering herbal/chemical thing going on then it doesn’t matter how “clean” it is.  This one is pretty mild and doesn’t leave my mouth tasting funky for an hour after, which is a win.

Texture is smooth (not gritty) and it does foam a little, just don’t expect the same big foam as mainstream toothpaste.  For sensitivity, it’s been good enough that I’m not dreading cold water after brushing, but I’m also not going to claim it’s some miracle fix in a week.  It feels gentle on the gums and hasn’t irritated my mouth at all.

The 3 pack is what made it feel worth it to me since it’s easy to keep one in each bathroom and not run out unexpectedly.  If you need super sweet/minty toothpaste (or you’re buying for kids that only accept that candy mint taste), this may be a harder sell, but for an unsweet “clean” paste that’s actually usable day to day, I’d buy it again.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Party Pastel Rainbow Balloons Assorted, 90Pcs 12" Macaron Candy Colored Latex Balloons for Unicorn Birthday Party Wedding Engagement Anniversary Christmas or Family Party Decorations (Multicolor)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
These balloons arrived in a vacu-sealed bag. I have to say that was a pleasant surprise. None of the balloons were dry rotted (which happens a lot oddly enough) and silver ribbon was included. The pastel colors are exactly as presented in the advertisement photos.

Balloons are standard "party" balloon size and were easy to blow up and tie off. These did NOT pop easily at all.

Overall, price point was excellent, colors are what I wanted and packaging was well done.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are really pretty “macaron” pastel balloons. The colors are soft candy/rainbow (baby shower / unicorn vibes) and not loud neon, which is exactly what I wanted.

Count was correct (I got 90). They came in a sealed bag and didn’t have that weird dry-rot/rubber stink, and none of them felt brittle right out of the package. I used a cheap hand pump and they inflated evenly to a normal 12” size without fighting the necks, and the ones we tied off held air fine for the party (no random pops just from sitting there).

For durability, I’m not saying they’re “indestructible,” but they’re better than the super thin dollar-store balloons that seem to split while you’re tying them. For the price per balloon, I’d buy these again if you want a big pastel mix that actually behaves like a normal balloon and doesn’t waste half the bag.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Pearlicare Kids Replacement Brush Heads (8 Pack) - Compatible With Phillips Sonicare Kid Electric Toothbrush, 2 Series, 3 Series, Flexcare, DiamondClean, Plaque Control, ProResults, Gum Health
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are really nice, these fit the kids sonicare toothbrush and hold up well.  The 7 year old and 2 year old haven't had any issues while using.  No breaks and easy to change out.  Overall, work exactly as intended.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These fit our Sonicare Kids handle just fine and click on/off like the brand name heads. I was a little worried they’d be loose or rattle (we’ve had off-brand ones do that) but these have stayed put and haven’t popped off during brushing.

Brush feel is kid-friendly. The bristles are soft enough that my kid isn’t complaining about “scratchy” gums, but they still seem to clean well. Also no weird sharp plastic edges around the base like some cheap replacements.

I can’t speak to long-term lifespan yet, but for basic compatibility + being comfortable enough that a kid will actually use it, these have been a solid pass in our house.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Compare to Ensure. Amazon Basic Care NutriSure Original Strawberry Nutrition Shake is packed with 26 vitamins and minerals, plus 9 grams of protein, for balanced, complete nutrition in each one-serving bottle. Each ready-to-drink shake provides 50% of the daily value of Vitamin D to help support bone health. These single-serving nutrition shakes come in convenient, easy-to-open bottles, great to use at home or while on the go. These nutritional shakes are best when served cold. Enjoy balanced, complete nutrition with a pleasant strawberry-flavored shake, naturally and artificially flavored. This product is gluten free and suitable for those who are lactose intolerant; not for people with galactosemia. This product is not manufactured or distributed by the owner of the registered trademark Ensure.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
These are handy if you need to maintain a specific calorie count. At 220 per bottle that's a bit much unless you need a quick snack. I find that these have a bit of a chalky / filmy after taste. Not really bad but reminds me of milk and I don't care for that. My guy on the other hand LOVES these, swears they taste like "strawberry vanilla quick milkshake" .

Overall, these maybe useful but were not specifically for me. However, my other half these are great for.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I keep these around for days when someone needs calories/protein fast (or something to take meds with) and doesn’t want to deal with food. I’ve had Ensure and this is a really close match in thickness/texture and how “filling” it feels for a single bottle.

Strawberry flavor is the make-or-break part and it’s going to be person dependent. Served cold like they recommend, it’s totally drinkable for me and doesn’t have that super fake candy taste, but it does have that nutrition-shake aftertaste that hangs around a bit. My guy is pickier about that and he’ll drink it, but it’s not something he reaches for unless he needs it.

If you like Ensure style shakes and just need something you can consistently get down without gagging on chalky grit, these do the job. If you’re hoping it tastes like a regular strawberry milk, you’re probably going to be disappointed.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: DetraPel Fabric & Clothing Protector - 5oz (150ml) - As Seen on Shark Tank
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: I have white, yeah I know this is a self inflicted issue, seat cushions at my dining room table.  This so far, has protected against tiny Cheetos fingers trying to climb up to sit at the table.  I wish the spray bottle worked better for the application.  I was only able to treat 2 chairs before the dispenser bottle broke.  Now I have some left in the can but can't use it like I want.  So 5 stars for the actual item 1 star for the dispenser.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I bought the 5oz bottle to test on a couple things before I go spraying a bunch of clothes/furniture. I did two dining chairs first and honestly the product itself seems to do what it’s supposed to do, but the spray nozzle on my bottle is the weak link and it turns this into a “works but not worth owning” situation.

It did not last long before the sprayer started acting up (basically quit after those chairs). It won’t put out a consistent mist and then it just kind of dribbles/streams so you can’t apply it evenly. That matters with something like this because if you can’t control the spray you end up wasting product and missing spots, and I’m not interested in babying a bottle or transferring it to another sprayer.

So for effectiveness I’m fine with it from what I saw, but build quality on the delivery mechanism is a no for me. If they fix the sprayer I’d try it again, but as-is I wouldn’t buy this one again.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: UCAN Performance Energy Powder + Protein Packets with SuperStarch - Vanilla Flavor - 12 Count
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: The flavor on this is similar to "ensure" if you like that thicker creamer vanilla then this is very tasty.  If you do not care for that texture then you won't like this.  It mixes well with water or as a smoothie like advertised.  I tried it both ways.  The vanilla isn't overly sweet , but it is sweeter then I personally care for.  This is a mild boost at best and didn't give me the jitters at all.  My largest issue with this is the price point.  For what I am getting I can eat a "bar" or make a drink with other blends for 1/2 the cost.  For travel, maybe this is great but other then that not much to be overly thrilled about.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I grabbed the vanilla UCAN Performance Energy + Protein packets for workout days and for those times I need something more “real” than an energy drink but don’t want a full meal sitting heavy. The only thing I really cared about was: does it give me a steady lift without the crash/jitters and without my stomach getting weird.

For me it’s been a solid “gentle but effective” boost. It feels more even than a lot of pre-workout / sugary stuff and I don’t get that wired feeling. I also didn’t get the gross sloshy stomach feeling I can get from some powders if I drink them and then move around right away.

Flavor is fine. It’s vanilla but not like a dessert vanilla… more like a mild vanilla shake vibe. Not overly sweet and no nasty lingering aftertaste. Texture wise it’s not chalk-free (most powders aren’t), but it’s very drinkable and hasn’t been hard to get down.

Price wise, these make sense to me for packets because they’re easy to throw in a bag and I’m not wasting product. If you’re using this daily it’ll add up fast, but for “workout days / need fuel but don’t want caffeine” days it’s been worth it and I would buy again.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: GDbow Breathalyzer Portable Digital Alcohol Tester for Personal Use -White
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
Not kidding about the lanyard it was a challenge when completely sober can't imagine trying to put in on after a few drinks.

This is a pretty small (very portable) test that is great for for a baseline. I don't know if it is 100% accurate since I don't have a police issued breathalyzer to compare it to. However, since I live in an area that drinking is pretty much at every event this is good to have on hand as a "check your self" reminder.

You will need 2 AAA batteries, this doesn't come with any. It is pretty small and will fit in your pocket or purse. Overall, Perfect for me to put in my "finally able to drink" child's glove box and remind them the house rule. Don't care the time if you / your friends need a ride call me no questions asked no trouble given. No excuses.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I got this as a personal “do I need to think about calling an Uber” check to keep in the glove box. Setup is simple BUT it is not ready to use out of the box in the way I expected. Mine needed batteries (not included), so until I had those it was basically a paperweight. If you’re buying this for emergencies, order batteries at the same time.

Once powered up it’s easy enough: turn it on, wait for the warm up, then blow for the time it wants. Just be aware there are some little operating rules you won’t appreciate until you’re actually trying to use it after a couple drinks (warm up time, it will error out if you don’t blow steady/long enough, and you can’t rush back-to-back tests without waiting). In a calm setting it’s fine, but it’s a bit fiddly for “impaired brain” moments.

As far as accuracy goes, I’m not going to pretend I can validate that without a known calibrated unit to compare it to. I treat this as a rough baseline/reminder tool, not something I’d ever use to decide I’m “good to drive.” For me it’s useful as a safety check that nudges the conservative decision.

Overall: convenient enough to keep on hand and use, but plan on the battery situation and don’t buy this thinking it’s going to be lab-precise or that you can mindlessly operate it when you’re not 100% sharp.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Beschoi Professional Salon Hair Straightener and Curler 2 in 1，Ceramic Tourmaline Ionic 3D Floating Plate with LCD 180℉-450℉, Adjustable Temperature & Dual Voltage, Auto Shut Down
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
This isn't the greatest straightener for thick curly hair. It does have the ability to adjust the heat temp, which is useful. However, the plates are rather small and just don't work great on my hair. It is packaged well and oddly does curl my hair better then straightening is.

The power button and digital screens are easy to see and clear to read however, please note, that when turning it on it isn't instant you MUST hold the power button longer then other product types or it simply won't turn on. Not a major issue but still an issue.

The 360 swivel cord, the glove and clips are all excellent features. However, overall this isn't a very well made curler/straightener.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I bought this because I wanted one hot tool that can straighten AND do quick loose curls/waves without digging through multiple things in the bathroom.

The pass/fail for me was simple: does it straighten without having to do a ton of tiny sections, and can I tell what temp I’m actually on without guessing. On my fine/medium, frizz-prone hair it passes. I can do a pass or two and it comes out smooth and shiny, and it doesn’t have that “dragging / ripping” feel some cheaper irons have.

On my teen’s thicker hair, this is where the limits show. It will straighten it, but it’s not fast, and the plate size doesn’t feel like it’s grabbing enough hair per pass to make it a time saver. So if you have thick/curly hair and you’re expecting salon-speed straightening, I wouldn’t buy it for that.

For the “2 in 1” part: it does loose waves/curls fine once you get the motion down, but it’s not replacing a real curling iron if you want consistent curls. The LCD temp display is nice and the adjustable temp is actually useful.

Usability note because it matters day-to-day: the power/temp buttons are easy to bump while you’re using it, and the power button needs a longer press than I expected (once you know it, it’s fine, but it wasn’t obvious the first time).

Overall, for normal straightening and occasional waves on fine/medium hair, it does what I needed. If your main goal is speeding through very thick hair, I’d skip and get a wider plate tool.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Liquid Protein 2Go Hydrolyzed by Aminorip. 26 Grams Protein, 2.5 oz, No Fat, Sugar Free, No Carbs. Ready to Drink. Predigested Hydrolysate Supplement. On The Go. Supports Endurance (Strawberry))
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are pretty sweet in flavor for being sugar free and Keto friendly.  Easy to drink no weird after flavor, overall think drinking a liquid fruit roll-up.  Mixes well in shakes or taking as it.  Price point is very reasonable .  Overall, does what it says and doesn't taste bad at all.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I grabbed these Liquid Protein 2Go shots for days I’m running around and don’t want to deal with powder, a shaker, and then having to wash it. For that purpose these are great — tiny 2.5oz bottle, twist cap, toss it in a purse/gym bag/car and it’s ready to go. No mixing, no clumps, no gritty texture.

Taste wise the strawberry is actually pretty decent for a sugar free / no carb protein. It’s sweet enough to get it down and it doesn’t have that nasty lingering chemical aftertaste that a lot of these “on the go” shots have. The texture is smooth and more like a thicker drink than a gel, so it’s not hard to swallow.

As far as performance, it does what I bought it for: an easy way to get 26g of protein in fast when I’m not going to sit and make a real shake/meal. I wouldn’t call it a “replacement meal” or anything, but it’s been a solid add-on for workouts/travel.

Price is the only real sting. It’s convenient, but you’re definitely paying for the ready-to-drink part compared to buying a tub of protein. If you’re going to use it daily, do the math first. For me as an emergency/keep-on-hand option, it’s worth it.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Hydration Energy Blend by Aminorip. Hydration Supplement with Sodium, Potassium, No Caffeine, 500 mg Electrolytes, 15 Servings, Reduce Muscle Cramps, Increase Energy & Endurance (22 oz, Lemonade)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
Really like the headline says, if you can not have a lot of carbs or don't want them in your diet then this isn't the drink for you. Some day's I need to have a large intake of carbs yet due to allergies what I would need to eat would be MASSIVE so bam I can drink this and I am good to go.

Flavor isn't bad I enjoy it.

Like every other powder mix I have ever tried the best way to get this to mix well is take 1 to 2 oz of hot water, dump the powder in and shake/stir until dissolved then add super cold water and you shouldn't have any clumping or powder balls to deal with.

No caffeine is great since I usually have to take this at night and I don't want to be up all night.

Price point, For ME, isn't bad since I am not taking this daily. Overall, it does exactly what it says it will do but I highly recommend reading the label to make sure it will work with your diet choices.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I bought this because I wanted an electrolyte mix I can take at night (or late evening) without caffeine messing up my sleep. The “energy” wording worried me but this is actually stimulant-free, so that part is a win for me.

Real world use: after a heavy sweat day I mixed it up and chugged it. It will dissolve in cold water but I did get a little bit of powder wanting to float, so what worked best was a splash of warm water first to break it up, stir/shake, then top off with cold water/ice. Once I did it that way it was smooth and easy enough to make.

Taste is very lemonade / salty electrolyte drink. Not the worst I’ve had, but it’s not something I’d sip for fun either. This is one of those “drink it because you need it” mixes. Stomach-wise it didn’t bother me.

For results, it did what I wanted it to do (felt better the next morning and didn’t get that crampy feeling). Value is just okay: 15 servings goes fast, and if you’re using it daily you’ll burn through the tub. I ended up using about a half serving on normal days and saving the full serving for the really sweaty days, and that makes the price make more sense.

Overall, if you need a no-caffeine electrolyte option and you’re fine with a salty lemonade flavor, this is a solid buy. If you’re expecting a sweet “sports drink” taste, you probably won’t love it.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Energy Gummies Vitamin B12 1000mcg Methylcobalamin Methyl B12 + Folic Acid Energy Supplement – Natural Raspberry Flavor - Vegan, Pectin Based, Non-GMO, Gluten Free
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are pretty standard gummies, no funky or chalky after flavor.  They don't get stuck or pull on your teeth while chewing.  Price point is a bit high for VitB12 BUT they are gummies and with the coupon not to much higher.  IF you prefer taking supplements in gummy form vs pill then it is probably a good investment of your money.  However, if it isn't a bit deal to you then it might not be cost effective.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are a nice option if you specifically need a B12 gummy and don’t want to swallow another pill. The chew is pectin based so it’s not that weird rubbery gelatin texture, and the raspberry flavor is actually pleasant. Not candy-sweet and I didn’t get that lingering “vitamin” aftertaste that makes you regret taking it.

For the “energy” claim I’m always skeptical because if you’re not actually low on B12 you may not notice anything. For me it’s more of a steady/fine feeling vs some noticeable jolt, which is what I prefer anyway (no jitters, no crash). If you’re expecting this to feel like caffeine, it won’t.

My only hesitation is price/value compared to a basic B12 pill. Gummies are almost always a premium, so I’d only keep buying these if you (or a family member) realistically won’t take tablets. If you’re already fine with pills, you can probably get the same ingredient for less money.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Crutcheze Odor Destroying Spray - Medical, pet, Shoe, Active wear Deodorizer
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: My child can clear a room with her stinky shoes.  It's a known fact that she has to leave her trainers outside.  This spray really helps cut down/out the sweat scent.  When you first spay it there is a bit of a chemical smell but it went away after a few minutes and didn't remain in the shoes.  Overall, ANY product that can help with those shoes gets 5 stars.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I bought this for the kind of stink that makes you want to throw the shoes away (kids sneakers after being worn all day). This works. A few sprays inside each shoe, let them sit and dry, and the next day the “clear a room” smell was basically gone. That’s the only test I really care about and it passed it.

Scent wise, it does have that temporary “spray/chemical” smell when it’s wet, but once it dries it doesn’t leave the shoes smelling like perfume or cleaner. No sticky residue inside the shoe either, and I didn’t have to wipe anything out after (just spray, dry, done). If you’re trying to save a pair of shoes or keep gym stuff from stinking up a closet, this is an easy fix.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Vital Performance Recover Drink 12 Pack - 24oz Bottles, 8g EAA, 5g BCAA, 20g Collagen per Bottle (BlackBerry Lime)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: As far as flavors go this is not something I want in my mouth because you can taste the salt going back into your body.  For results, this is one of the best recovery drinks I have ever used, I feel great after chugging it down.  If you are super active/ sick and sweating a ton then this is the drink for you.  But again, the flavor of this is not something anyone will drink unless they need it.  Price point seem high, but you can really split this into two uses if only doing a standard workout.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I’ve gone through a few of these Vital Performance Recover drinks (Blackberry Lime) after workouts and I’m kind of split: the recovery side feels legit, but the flavor is a compliance issue.

Taste wise it isn’t overly sweet, but it has that “you can taste the salts/minerals going back into your body” thing going on and it lingers. If you’re someone who can sip 24oz like flavored water, you might be fine, but I can’t just casually drink a whole bottle in one go.

What’s worked for me is treating one bottle like two servings (half now, half later). Doing it that way makes it way more tolerable and it also makes the price feel a little less painful, because a 12-pack of these isn’t cheap if you’re using one every single workout.

Overall: if you’re sweating a lot / doing harder sessions and you actually need the recovery support, these do the job. If you’re picky about aftertaste, I’d think twice because the flavor isn’t something you “forget is there.”

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: PACKQUEEN 25 Gift Boxes 4x4x4 Inches, Paper Gift Boxes with Lids for Crafting, Gift Ornaments, Cupcakes, Candles, Wedding Favor Boxes, Glossy White, Textured Finish
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are your basic pastry /  jewelry / take home box for light weight or delicate gifts.  These are not designed to store heavy items in them, if you do put something with any noticeable weight in them you will need to support the bottoms at all times or the box may break open.  Decent size and easy to put together.  Overall, great to keep on hand for quick gifts.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are good little boxes and the 4x4x4 size is accurate. They come flat and you just fold/crease them into shape. Once you do the first one the rest go together fast and you don’t need tape or anything fancy, which is exactly what I wanted for quick “I need to wrap something small” situations.

The only real gate for me on these types of boxes is: can they handle normal handling without popping open. For lightweight stuff (small candles, ornaments, favors, etc) they’re totally fine and the lid stays on. If you’re putting anything with noticeable weight in there, you’ll want to support the bottom when you pick it up (same as most thin cardstock gift boxes). They don’t feel like thick rigid bakery boxes, but they also aren’t flimsy to the point of being unusable.

Finish is a nice glossy white with a bit of texture so they look clean/giftable without extra effort. Overall, these are “good enough to stop thinking about” for small gifts/crafts and I’d buy them again for that use.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: PACKQUEEN 10 Gift Boxes 8x8x4 inches, Gift Boxes with Lids for Light Weight Gifts, Crafting, Cupcake Boxes, Glossy White
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: Like the box advertise's these are not designed for heavy gifts.  The boxes, are super easy to assemble.  The card board / card-stock is decent for jewelry ./ bakery items etc.  These will dent/ get crease marks or bent if not stored flat.  Overall, these are what I expected.  They didn't rip with normal handling which is nice.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are decent sized boxes (8x8x4) and they go together fast. They fold/assemble easy, the glossy white looks clean and “gift-y” without having to wrap them, and the lids fit on fine and stay on for normal carrying (nothing popping open on me).

Just be aware these are for LIGHT weight items. The failure point is the bottom/edges if you put anything with real weight in it — the rule with these is you need to keep a hand under the bottom when carrying or it will start to bow and can pop open. For cupcakes, cookies, small crafts, or a light gift they held up and didn’t rip under normal handling, but I wouldn’t trust them for a heavy candle or mug unless you plan on supporting it the whole time.

Also, store them flat. The glossy finish shows creases/dents and once they get mashed they don’t look “new” again. Overall, good for what they are, just don’t expect heavy duty boxes.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Back Pain Cold Reusable Ice Pack Belt Therapy for Lower Lumbar, Sciatic Nerve Pain Relief Degenerative Disc Disease Coccyx Tailbone Pain Reusable Gel Flexible Medical Grade Ther-X1 Gel
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
This is really great. I am 5'9" 180lbs & my guy is 6' 250lbs both of us have been able to use this belt. It is wide enough that it sits high on the torso and low enough on the back that no matter your build your entire lower back is covered.

Very large area of Velcro to hold it in place (think WWE Wrestling belt shape). The gel pack is easy it remove and either freeze OR warm up. The pouch is also Velcro close so this cold/hot pack wont come out easily if you move around.

Overall, for this price point this is one of the better treatments we have used for sore backs.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I got this for lower back / sciatic flare ups and also because sitting too long at a desk chair makes my tailbone cranky. This is basically a reusable gel ice pack built into a belt, which is exactly what I needed because balancing a loose ice pack back there is annoying and it never stays put.

It gets cold fast in the freezer and it still stays flexible enough to wrap around you instead of turning into a stiff brick. The belt is wide with strong velcro and it holds like a WWE belt (in a good way). The pouch also closes with velcro so the pack doesn’t slowly slide out when you stand up/walk around, which is the whole make-or-break feature for me. Coverage is solid on the entire lower back area and it fits me and my other half fine without it feeling like it’s only made for one body type.

Comfort wise, it’s cold enough to actually calm things down but the belt keeps it from being that direct “freezer burn” feeling on one spot. If you’re in a flare and need hands-free icing while you’re doing stuff around the house (or just trying to sit without wanting to cry), this is way easier than juggling towels and straps.

Price point feels fair since it’s reusable and I can get multiple sessions out of it before it fully warms up, then just toss it back in the freezer. Overall it does what it says and the fact that it stays in place is what makes it worth owning.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: 2 Pack Portable Pill Case for Purse or Pocket 6 Compartments Travel Pill Organizer Small Cute Pill Box Dispenser Daily Medication Carry Case Travel Supplement Holder Container
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
I use these for all kinds of things besides just medications of my own. I ALWAYS have to have allergy medication or Benadryl or random 'kids' fever etc. meds and I store them in these in bags. I just label the compartments and toss meds in them and go.

Also, these work great as coin storage, lego storage, random candy storage and any other odd small thing that can be lost in a purse, diaper bag, game bag, cosplay bag you name it bag!

Well made and durable. Also, labels do stick to these or you can use a sharpie.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are small but in a good way. I wanted something I could toss in my purse (or even a pocket) and not have it take up space, and these do that. The 6 compartments are enough for my daily vitamins plus a couple “as needed” meds, and they don’t all end up mixed together as long as you don’t overstuff the slots.

The main thing I was worried about was the lids popping open in a bag and so far that hasn’t happened. Mine snap shut with a solid click and I’ve had them rolling around with keys/chapstick and they’ve stayed closed. The hinges and plastic feel better than the flimsy dollar store ones that crack fast, and I like that you get 2 so one can live in the purse and the other in the car/desk.

I’ve also been using one for non-pill stuff (a couple earrings and tiny hair ties) and it works great for that too, so it’s actually been more useful than I expected. Overall these are simple, portable, and seem like they’ll hold up to daily carry.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Orthotic Insoles 1/2 Length - High Arch Supports Shoe Insert for Plantar Fasciitis Foot Pain Relief, Flat Feet Pronation, Heel Spur,Shin Splints Relief for Women and Men,Heel Stability,Height Increase
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: These are pretty good as build ups for older work boots that the old inserts are going out in.  My guy put these in (as a replacement for the old ones) then put in soft inserts over them.  He has pretty intense flare ups of his plater fasciitis every now and then and he likes these double stacked when it does happen.  This insert won't work best for everyone since it is hard not soft.  BUT if you need to have a hard surface to press on then its a great one.  It fits his 11 size boots with no issues and are comfortable for him.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
Got these for plantar fasciitis flare ups and for shoes where I don’t want to rip out the factory insole. The 1/2 length design is the whole point here and it does what it’s supposed to do — heel + arch support without eating up the toe box, so my sneakers still fit like normal.

Support wise these are NOT subtle. The arch is high and it’s a firm “push” under the arch/heel, more like a hard support base than anything squishy. For me that’s exactly what helps when my heel/arch is angry, but if you’re looking for soft cushioning or you hate feeling an arch there at all, you probably won’t like these. I can tell pretty quick if an insert is actually doing anything, and with these I feel the support immediately when I stand/walk.

Comfort depends on your foot. On my feet it’s tolerable (and helpful) in shorter bursts, but I wouldn’t call it a “forget it’s there” insert. These are more of a targeted pain-relief / stability type insert than an all-day plush comfort thing. Overall, they do what I bought them for, but know going in they’re firm and very high-arch.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Gift Bags
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: I am really surprised at the quality of these bags.  They are better then average (what you would normally find in a dollar store) and probably along the lines of what you expect to find in a party store.  Really good quality.  IF you put something super heavy in them you should still support the bottom, but you would do that with any bag.  The designs and colors are fantastic.  Perfect for pretty much any event and most holidays.  Nice average size bags.  Would recommend.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are basic gift bags but they’re nicer then the thin dollar store ones that rip/crease the second you try to slide a box in. The paper is sturdier, the sides hold their shape, and the ribbon handles are actually attached well (no popping off when you pick it up).

I’ve put a heavier candle gift in one and wasn’t uncomfortable carrying it around without supporting the bottom, and for normal party gifts/toys they’re totally fine. If you’re stuffing something super heavy/sharp-cornered in there you’ll want to support the bottom like any bag, but for what most people use gift bags for these hold up well and don’t feel “one and done.”

The designs/colors are also the kind you can use for multiple occasions (birthdays, random holidays, last minute gifts) so they’re easy to keep on hand. Overall, better quality than the cheap packs and I’d buy them again.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Elderberry Gummies for Kids & Adults Immune Support - Vitamin C Echinacea Propolis & Sambucus Black Elderberry Gummy Vitamins - Organic Nature Booster Non-GMO Gluten Free - USA…
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: You get 2 bottles (so 2 month supply) for under $20 (at the time of this review).  Even the generic at my local pharmacy and big box stores are more expensive then that.  These gummies didn't have a bitter or tart after taste and I didn't find them to be overly sticky/hard.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are pretty standard vitamin gummies but the main thing for me is: do they taste normal enough that people will actually take them every day. And yes, these do. They’re sweet (as expected) but not in that overly fake/cough-syrup elderberry way, and there isn’t a weird chalky vitamin aftertaste hanging around. Texture is also fine — not rock hard and not so sticky they glue to your teeth.

On the money side, the bottle is basically a 30 day supply if you follow the serving size, so I’d compare the price to other “immune” gummies by thinking of it as cost per month, not just cost per bottle. At the price I bought them at it felt reasonable for what it is, especially since nobody in my house is avoiding taking them because of the flavor. I’d buy them again if you want an easy gummy option and you’re picky about taste/aftertaste.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Makryn Adjustable Plantar Fasciitis Feet Inserts-Orthotic Shoe Insoles-Arch Support for Men&Women-Flat Feet,High Arches,Relieve Metatarsal,Arch and Heel Pain(Green, X-Large)
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text: My guy likes these in his sneakers not hit work boots.  He finds these to be more comfortable in a lighter weight shoe.  He does have planter fasciitis and it does flare up randomly like many but he uses this insert all the time.  He does have low arches but isn't flat footed.  They were easy to trim to fit his shoes, he wears a size 12 and has the inserts in a XL size.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
I got the green XL for my everyday sneakers because my heels/arches have been angry lately (lots of standing) and I needed something with more structure than the squishy “comfort” insoles.

These do give real support, but they are not a drop-in-and-forget-it insert. The adjustable arch piece is the whole point and it actually matters — one foot needs more help than the other and being able to tweak the height made a noticeable difference for me. The first wear was a little “too much” in the arch until I backed it off, then it felt supportive instead of like a hard lump.

Comfort wise, once I had the arch set right, they were fine for daily wear and I didn’t feel like I had to rip my shoes off halfway through the day. They are on the firmer side (which is what I wanted for pain), so if you like super soft gel this isn’t that.

Big heads up: fit depends a lot on the shoe. In my roomier sneakers they sit fine and don’t slide around, but in tighter shoes they take up space and can make the toe box feel cramped. So I’d say these are best for sneakers/work shoes that already have some room, not slim fashion shoes.

Overall, for plantar/heel pain these have been helpful for me once adjusted, just expect a little trial-and-error the first couple wears to get them set how your feet actually tolerate.

AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.product_description: Elderberry Gummies with Zinc Vitamin C
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.review_text:
You get a 30 day supply (60 gummies at 2 per serving) in this bottle. Currently, at the time of this review, it is $12.99 and there is a coupon for a BOGO offer so 2 bottles for $12.99. Typically, a 30 day supply of gummies like this from my local chain pharmacy (their generic brand) is $16.99 when BOGO is going on.

These are easy to chew and didn't get stuck in my teeth. Decent taste and have the same listed amount of ingredients and percentages of what you are intaking as my normal daily that I take.
AEOK4TQIKGO23SJKZ6PW4FETNNDA_1.best_prediction_review:
These are pretty standard elderberry gummies, but I actually like them. Flavor is more “berry” and less that weird vitamin/chemical thing, and the texture is soft/chewy without turning into glue on your teeth. No gross aftertaste lingering either, which is usually what makes me stop taking gummies.

I also like that it’s just easy to remember and use. The bottle is straightforward, gummies aren’t stuck together, and I don’t have to force myself to swallow pills. As far as what I can realistically judge, the label amounts for the zinc/vit C are in line with what I normally take during the season when everyone is coughing/sneezing, so I’m comfortable using these as my daily “support” option.

Price-wise, I grabbed these with a coupon and that’s what makes them worth it for me. At full price I’d probably just buy a basic vitamin C + zinc from my local big box store and call it a day, but for a gummy that I’ll actually take consistently, the cost made sense. I’d buy again if the deal is there.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: AyurForest Karela Powder, Momordica charantia | Bitter Melon | Bitter Gourd 1.41 oz 40 GMS, All Natural Non-GMO, Balanced Blood Sugar Level & Boosting Immunity
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: This seemed a bit strong for my system - it takes very little so perhaps use additional caution when first starting to try this out in order to determine your personal tolerance. A little goes a long way. Personally this isn't something I use often because it's tough to find the right dose level. I also wish it were organic but having said that, there are clear benefits to the use of this and since it can be difficult to find in season, I really like the ability to find it in a powder form. I would buy again and enjoy the size - plenty to use without waste but not over done as it takes a minimal amount to get effect.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
This is exactly what it says it is: straight karela/bitter melon powder. I bought it because I’m trying to keep a couple of “use as needed” blood sugar supports on hand, and I prefer single-ingredient powders over blends where you can’t really control what you’re getting per serving.

The first quick check for me was whether it seemed like a real, usable powder (not clumpy, not obviously cut with anything, and not stale). Mine showed up as a fine, dry green powder and the bag was sealed properly. I started small (about 1/8 tsp) once a day with food for a few days to see how my system handled it, then moved up a bit. Taken that way it’s been gentle enough on my stomach, and I’ve noticed a modest but real improvement in post-meal “sluggishness” that I tend to get when I’m eating more carbs than usual. I’m not treating this as a medication, but it’s been a useful tool.

For the amount you get (40 g), this isn’t something you’ll fly through if you dose realistically, so the value makes sense to me. My only real knock is I wish it were certified organic (especially for something I’m using regularly), because “natural/non-GMO” isn’t the same bar. If you already use bitter melon and want a simple powder you can titrate, this one has been solid so far and I’d buy it again.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: When you think of Vitamin C, Amla fruit probably doesn’t come to mind. But it should! Amla, a fruit native to India, is a good source of Vitamin C. In fact, Super Organics Amla Powder, or Indian Gooseberry as it’s more commonly known, contains 11% of your recommended daily intake of Vitamin C, supplying your body with added support for healthy immunity.Even better, all Super Organics products are USDA Certified Organic, Vegan & Non-GMO, ensuring you fill your body with only high-quality, nature-made ingredients that have been carefully cultivated for maximum benefits. Supercharge your health with Super Organics convenient and versatile superfood powders!These statements have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Association. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: I'd never taken astragalus before but heard it could help with immune function, energy etc so thought I'd give it a try. Initially I started almost by mistake as it was the main ingredient in another product I was taking...garlic of all things. I had taken the kyloric brand garlic in the past but suddenly found myself more energetic, alert and feeling good but couldn't figure out why. I realized I had bought a different garlic - it contained astragalus. That's when I realized the benefit. Fast forward a bit and now I use this as a stand alone. I like the amount, it's fine ground and easy to mix in without notice of anything. Good quality and would buy again!
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
I like the idea of getting some vitamin C support from a whole-food source instead of straight ascorbic acid, and this checked the main boxes for me (USDA organic/vegan/non-GMO), so I gave it a try. The make-or-break with powders is always whether I’ll actually use them and whether I can find a dose that doesn’t feel like “too much” for my system. For me it definitely takes less than you’d think. If I push it, it gets a little too astringent/tart and I’m less likely to reach for it, so I’ve landed closer to a small amount mixed into a smoothie or a little water/juice rather than trying to do a big spoonful.

Once I treated it like a “little goes a long way” powder, it became easy to work into a routine and I’ve been more consistent with it. I can’t honestly claim some dramatic immunity change from this alone (too many variables), but it’s a clean way to add amla without extra ingredients and it agrees with my stomach at the level I’m using. Value-wise, the bag lasts a while because the usable dose for me is small, so the cost per serving ends up being pretty reasonable compared to constantly buying fresh fruit or pricier blends. If you’re sensitive to tart/acidic things, start low and adjust—finding the right amount is the main “effort” with this one.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: Morning Drink Multi-Vitamin with Biotin for Hair Skin and Nails - 32 Fl Oz
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: I really like the ingredient list on this because it includes many/most of the things I try to actively ensure I take like b12 etc...in the form that I tolerate best. Many use inferior forms of b12 like cyanocobalamin but the methyl is far better tolerated and doesn't give me headaches like the other. The flavor is okay - tolerable. Not great. This claims high absorption and while I don't feel much different, it is fairly easy on the stomach and frankly, a lot easier than taking a bunch of pills. Make sure you shake it and keep refrigerated. I've added this to my list and would be inclined to buy again in the future.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
I like the idea of a multi + biotin in a morning drink because I’m more consistent with liquids than yet another pill, and the 32 oz bottle actually lasts long enough to feel practical. Taste is always the deal breaker for me with liquid vitamins and this one is…fine. Not amazing, but not that thick medicinal syrup that makes you dread it either, and it goes down a lot easier cold.

I’ve taken enough supplements to know I don’t do well with “mystery blends” that are heavy on extras, so I read the label pretty closely before I started. Nothing in here jumped out as an automatic no for me, but I still treated it like any new vitamin drink and started smaller for a few days just to make sure it agreed with me (some forms of B vitamins and certain sweeteners can give me headaches or stomach irritation if I go in full force). For me it’s been easy to tolerate, and I like that it’s one simple step in the morning instead of juggling multiple bottles.

I can’t claim some dramatic transformation overnight, but I do notice that on the weeks I’m actually taking this consistently (because it’s sitting in the fridge and I’ll actually use it), I’m less likely to fall into that “I forgot my vitamins again” pattern. If you already have a pretty dialed-in supplement routine, this may feel redundant, but if your main problem is compliance and you need something you’ll realistically take every day, this is a solid option as long as you’re ok with a just-okay taste.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: Best Earth Naturals Vision Support Formula Supplement with Eye Vitamins, Lutein, Vitamin A, Quercetin and More - 30 Count
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: I really like this blend for eye health but I'm less impressed by the overall junk that fills the supplements. I realize there are others that do the same but I don't seem to get the same bang for the buck with this one. Maybe it's because I take these already but ultimately these feel less helpful than normal. Would not buy again.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
I picked this up as a basic “insurance policy” eye support supplement (lots of screen time + getting older) and I’m a bit on the fence, mostly because I’m not convinced the payoff is strong enough for the price unless you already know you do well with this type of blend. The ingredient lineup is what initially sold me (lutein, vitamin A, quercetin, etc) and I like that it’s a 30 count so you can try it without committing to a huge bottle.

For me the gate with supplements is always: does it feel like a clean, efficient formula I’ll actually stick with, and does it justify being another monthly line item? I didn’t notice any clear, repeatable difference in eye comfort/strain in the short window I tried it (not saying it can’t help, just that I personally can’t certify it yet). I also tend to prefer products that keep the “noise” low (unnecessary fillers/add-ins), and while this isn’t the worst I’ve seen, it didn’t feel like a standout compared to other basic eye blends you can find for similar money.

I’m glad I started with the small count and I can finish the bottle, but I’m not sure I’d replace it when it’s gone unless I see a more obvious benefit over time or catch it at a better price.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: GenOne Labs Vegan Protein Powder, for Men and Women, Amazing Taste (Chocolate)
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: I tried both the chocolate and vanilla but ended up preferring the chocolate more than vanilla. I wish there was a way to measure the arsenic load but otherwise, nice product.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
I’m pretty picky about vegan chocolate protein because most either taste “earthy” or have that fake sweet aftertaste that hangs around and then I stop using it. This one is one of the few I can actually keep in rotation — the chocolate flavor is genuinely tolerable and doesn’t leave that plant/soil note that makes you dread the next shake.

The only real caveat for me is texture. It mixes fine in a shaker, but it’s on the thicker/heavier side (and it gets even heavier if you let it sit a few minutes), which for me is a compliance thing — if it feels too dense I just won’t reach for it. Easy fix though: I use a little more water/ice (or blend it) and it’s totally fine.

Ingredient/safety-wise, I always pay attention to what the protein source is with vegan powders (especially anything rice-based because of the general heavy metal concerns in that space). I didn’t have any stomach issues with this, but if you’re very sensitive or you’re trying to avoid certain ingredients/sweeteners, it’s still worth reading the label carefully before committing.

AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.product_description: Gen One Labs- Vegan Protein Powder, for Men and Women, Amazing Taste (Vanilla)
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.review_text: When it comes to protein powder both ingredient and flavor is essential. This does a good job in both categories with one caveat...I'm not crazy about the consistency. In fact, I find it a bit heavy which ultimately makes me less inclined to use it. I know most of these are watery so it sounds absurd to complain about the heavy consistency but it's too much...fortunately it's easy to remedy so still gets high marks.
AGLCYFK2SZYTSYVMHA744ZC6AXBQ.best_prediction_review:
I bought this mainly because I wanted a simple vegan vanilla protein that didn’t taste like chalk and didn’t have a bunch of “mystery” additives. Taste is genuinely good for a plant protein — more like a light vanilla than a fake-cupcake sweet, and it blends pretty easily in a shaker with oat milk. In just water it’s still drinkable but the texture is a bit thicker/grainier (pretty normal for vegan powders in my experience). No weird aftertaste and it didn’t bother my stomach, which is a big deal for me since some protein powders leave me bloated.

As far as results, it does what I need it to do: an easy way to add protein when I’m short on time, especially on days I’m not getting enough from food. I’m not using it as a meal replacement, just a supplement, and it’s been a convenient option post-workout or as an afternoon “don’t grab junk” option.

My only real hang-up is that the description leans hard on “amazing taste” but doesn’t tell you much else. I still want to see a very clear ingredient list, protein per serving, sweetener type, and ideally some third-party testing info. If you’re sensitive to certain sweeteners or gums, you’ll want to double check the label before committing.

Price-wise it’s not the cheapest vegan protein out there, but if the ingredients are clean and consistent (and so far it has been for me), I can justify it because I’ll actually use it instead of forcing it down. I’d buy again, just wish they gave more transparency up front.
